item.title: Stellantis创新主管力求在战略重置期间保持高水平专利申请
item.description: 汽车巨头Stellantis首席创新官安妮·拉利隆周二表示，尽管该公司作为战略重置的一部分取消了许多电动汽车项目，但仍计划保持高水平的专利申请。【原文：Car giant Stellantis plans to keep a ​high level of patent ‌filings despite the cancellation of many electric vehicle projects as ​part of a ​strategic reset, its chief of ⁠innovation Anne Laliron said ​on Tuesday.】

item.title: 中国网约车平台WeRide因伊朗冲突扩大暂停迪拜自动驾驶出租车服务
item.description: 中国自动驾驶企业文创出行已暂停其在迪拜的无人驾驶出租车车队运营。随着美国与以色列对伊朗冲突的升级，中东关键枢纽地区局势动荡，而这些地区正是无人驾驶出租车行业最具发展前景的市场。【原文：Chinese autonomous driving firm WeRide has suspended its robotaxi fleet in Dubai as the ​escalating U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran roiled key Middle Eastern ‌hubs that have emerged as the robotaxi industry's most promising markets.】

item.title: 丰田的收购协议对埃利奥特而言是比公司治理更大的胜利
item.description: 丰田汽车公司决定进一步提高对集团旗下丰田工业公司的收购报价，这标志着激进投资基金埃利奥特投资管理公司取得了胜利。该基金数月来一直敦促这家汽车制造商大幅提高收购价格。【原文：Toyota's decision to further sweeten its bid for group company Toyota Industries marks a win for activist fund Elliott Investment Management, ​which had pushed the automaker for months for a heftier bump up in price.】

item.title: 越南经济蓬勃发展，但外国资金正从股市撤离
item.description: 越南正站在加入新兴市场俱乐部的门槛上，股市创下数年来的最大涨幅，但外国投资者却在抛售股票，并表示关税风险、持股限制以及某家企业对指数的垄断地位都阻碍了投资。【原文：Vietnam is on the cusp of joining the emerging-markets club and stocks have notched their biggest rally in years, but foreigners have been sellers and say investing is handicapped by tariff risks, ownership limits and one firm's dominance of the index.】

item.title: 华尔街期货下跌，中东冲突加剧通胀担忧
item.description: 美国股市期货下跌，因投资者评估美以对伊朗加强打击的后续影响，并为能源价格飙升可能推高通胀的可能性做好准备。【原文：U.S. stock ​index futures fell as investors assessed the fallout of intensified U.S. and Israeli strikes ‌on Iran and braced for the possibility that a surge in energy prices could drive inflation higher.】

item.title: 美元重拾魔力——但仅因缺乏替代选择
item.description: 尽管人们很想认为，自周末伊朗袭击事件以来，美元久违的"避险"需求已然回归，但实际情况并非如此简单明了，更多是受能源板块相对表现的影响。然而，市场反应所传递的信号可能同样具有深远影响。【原文：While it's tempting to assume the dollar's long-lost "safety" bid has returned since the weekend Iran attacks, it's not as clear-cut as it seems and owes more to relative energy plays. Yet the implications of the market response may be just as powerful.】

item.title: 《金融时报》报道，贝莱德支持的财团寻求在不包含巴拿马资产的情况下完成和记黄埔港口交易
item.description: 据《金融时报》周二报道，在巴拿马当局扣押相关资产后，由贝莱德支持的财团正推动完成对和记黄埔全球港口业务的收购，但不包括巴拿马的两个码头。【原文：A BlackRock-backed consortium ​is pushing to complete its acquisition of ‌CK Hutchison's global ports business without two terminals in Panama, after authorities seized the assets, Financial Times reported ​on Tuesday.】

item.title: 海湾地区机场关闭后，亚欧航线机票价格飙升
item.description: 由于美以联军对伊朗发动战争，中东主要枢纽机场关闭，亚洲至欧洲航线机票价格飙升，多家航空公司官网显示多条热门航线机票已售罄数日。【原文：The price of flights between Asia and ​Europe has soared after the closure of key Middle Eastern hubs due to the U.S.-Israel war against Iran, with airline websites showing tickets ‌on many popular routes booked out for days.】

item.title: 《金融时报》报道称，派拉蒙以1100亿美元收购华纳兄弟的交易有望获得美国联邦通信委员会支持
item.description: 美国联邦通信委员会主席布伦丹·卡尔已表示，该监管机构不会阻止派拉蒙以1100亿美元收购华纳兄弟的交易，并淡化了CBS与CNN合并引发的竞争担忧，英国《金融时报》周二报道称。【原文：U.S. Federal Communications Commission Chair ​Brendan Carr has signaled ‌that the watchdog will not seek ​to block Paramount's $110 ​billion deal to buy ⁠Warner Bros and ​played down competition ​concerns over a combination of CBS and CNN, ​the Financial Times ​reported on Tuesday.】

item.title: 日本季度资本支出增长6.5%，政府力图刺激投资
item.description: 日本财务省周二公布的数据显示，去年第四季度日本工厂和设备支出同比增长6.5%，表明强劲的投资需求持续支撑着勉强增长的经济。【原文：Japanese spending on factories and equipment rose 6.5% ‌on-year in the fourth quarter, Ministry of Finance data showed on Tuesday, indicating resilient investment demand continued to support a barely growing economy.】

item.title: 油价上涨，因美以与伊朗冲突扩大加剧供应风险
item.description: 周二油价连续第三日上涨，因美国与以色列对伊朗的冲突不断升级，以及霍尔木兹海峡航运面临的威胁，加剧了市场对中东关键产油区供应中断的担忧。【原文：Oil prices rose for a third day on Tuesday as the widening U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran and threats to shipping through the Strait of Hormuz heightened fears of supply disruptions from the key Middle ​East producing region.】

item.title: 据彭博社报道，Thoma Bravo即将达成收购WWEX的交易，将组建一家市值120亿美元的航运科技公司。
item.description: 彭博新闻周一援引知情人士消息报道，美国私募股权公司托马斯·布拉沃正就收购第三方物流服务商WWEX集团进行深入谈判，此举将缔造一家价值高达120亿美元的运输技术企业。【原文：U.S. private ​equity firm Thoma Bravo ‌is in advanced talks to buy third-party logistics provider ​WWEX Group to create ​a shipping technology business worth ⁠as much as $12 billion, ​Bloomberg News reported on ​Monday, citing people familiar with the matter.】

item.title: OpenAI首席执行官阿尔特曼表示，该公司正修改与五角大楼的协议。
item.description: OpenAI首席执行官山姆·阿尔特曼周一表示，这家ChatGPT开发商正与美国国防部合作，对双方协议进行部分调整。【原文：OpenAI Chief Executive Sam Altman said on Monday ​that the ChatGPT-maker is working ‌with the U.S. Department of Defense to make some changes ​in their agreement.】

item.title: 标普可能因野火将伯克希尔旗下太平洋电力公司降级至垃圾级
item.description: 标普全球周一表示，可能将伯克希尔哈撒韦旗下公用事业公司太平洋电力公司评级下调至垃圾级，因该公司因2020年俄勒冈州系列野火引发的集体诉讼导致负债激增。【原文：S&P Global said on Monday it may downgrade PacifiCorp, a utility owned by Berkshire Hathaway , to junk status as liabilities mount from class action ​litigation over a series of Oregon wildfires in 2020.】

item.title: 黑石820亿美元私募信贷基金出现净赎回
item.description: 全球最大另类资产管理公司黑石集团已同意在本季度提高其私募信贷基金的投资者分红，导致资金净流出，周一提交的证券文件显示。【原文：Blackstone , the world's largest alternative asset manager, has agreed to increase ​payouts to investors from a private credit fund ‌this quarter, leading to net outflows, a securities filing showed on Monday.】

item.title: 股市下跌，因中东冲突加剧通胀担忧
item.description: 周二亚洲早盘时段，股市再度下跌，美元走强，投资者正评估美国和以色列对伊朗的打击行动对能源价格及全球经济的影响。【原文：Stocks resumed their ​selloff and the dollar strengthened in early Asian trading on Tuesday as investors considered the implications ‌of U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran on energy prices and the global economy.】

item.title: 阿波罗、里昂·布莱克被控隐瞒与爱泼斯坦的商业关系而遭股东起诉
item.description: 股东周一提起拟议集体诉讼，控告阿波罗全球管理公司及其亿万富豪联合创始人莱昂·布莱克和马克·罗温，指控该私募资本公司近五年间持续欺诈股东，隐瞒其与性犯罪者、金融家杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的商业往来。【原文：Shareholders sued Apollo Global Management and ​its billionaire co-founders Leon Black and Marc Rowan on Monday in a proposed class action for ‌allegedly defrauding them for nearly five years about the private capital firm's business dealings with disgraced sex offender and financier Jeffrey Epstein.】

item.title: 亚马逊云计算部门称无人机袭击损毁阿联酋和巴林设施
item.description: 亚马逊云计算部门AWS周一表示，其位于阿联酋和巴林的设施受到无人机袭击影响，此次袭击发生在中东冲突期间。【原文：Amazon's ​cloud unit ‌AWS said ​on ​Monday its ⁠facilities ​in ​the UAE and ​Bahrain ​were impacted by ‌drone ⁠strikes amid ​the ​Middle ⁠East conflict.】

item.title: 日元、欧元承压，中东冲突加剧能源担忧
item.description: 周二，日元和欧元普遍走低，因中东冲突不断升级，市场将目光聚焦于依赖能源进口的国家，以及各国央行可能如何应对通胀压力。【原文：The yen and ​euro were broadly lower on Tuesday as the widening Middle East conflict focused attention on countries ‌dependent on energy imports and how central banks may respond to inflation pressures.】

item.title: 澳洲航空首席执行官表示，该公司拥有"相当完善"的燃油对冲机制，正密切监控油价飙升情况。
item.description: 澳洲航空首席执行官范妮莎·哈德森周二表示，该公司已实施"相当完善的"燃油对冲措施，但美国、以色列与伊朗之间的冲突导致油价飙升，这对航空业影响重大。【原文：Qantas Airways CEO Vanessa ​Hudson said on Tuesday ‌that the airline had "pretty good" fuel ​hedging in place ​but the spike in ⁠oil prices amid ​the conflict between ​the U.S., Israel and Iran was significant for the ​aviation industry.】

item.title: 伊朗誓言封锁霍尔木兹海峡，全球油气运输成本飙升
item.description: 周二航运数据和行业消息人士称，随着美国与伊朗冲突升级——德黑兰袭击了通过霍尔木兹海峡的船只——中东地区超级油轮的成本已创历史新高。【原文：Supertanker costs in the Middle East have hit ​all-time highs, according to shipping data and industry sources on Tuesday, as the U.S.-Iran conflict intensifies with Tehran attacking ships passing through the ‌Strait of Hormuz.】

item.title: 纽约法院拒绝推翻仲裁裁决，Venture Global公司胜诉壳牌公司
item.description: 纽约州最高法院周一驳回了壳牌公司撤销2025年8月作出的一项仲裁裁决的申请，该裁决支持了Venture Global公司的诉求。【原文：New York ‌state's Supreme ​Court ​on Monday ⁠denied ​Shell's petition ​to vacate an ​arbitral ​award issued ‌in ⁠favor of Venture ​Global ​in ⁠August 2025.】

item.title: 亚马逊数据中心部门收购乔治华盛顿大学弗吉尼亚校区
item.description: 亚马逊旗下亚马逊数据服务公司以4.27亿美元收购乔治华盛顿大学弗吉尼亚科技校区，该大学周一宣布。【原文：Amazon unit Amazon Data ​Services is acquiring George ‌Washington University's Virginia Science and Technology ​campus for $427 million, ​the university said on ⁠Monday.】

item.title: 美元重拾避险资产地位，伊朗袭击事件引发市场恐慌
item.description: 美国对伊朗发动袭击后美元的急剧反弹，让投资者确信该货币仍作为全球避风港发挥作用。随着中东地缘政治紧张局势加剧，美元正重拾其在危机时期的传统角色。【原文：The dollar's sharp rally following U.S. strikes on Iran is reassuring investors the currency still functions as a global safe-haven, with the greenback reclaiming its traditional crisis-era role as geopolitical tensions flare in ​the Middle East.】

item.title: 交易日：油价、收益率与不确定性齐涨
item.description: 油价和天然气价格创下数年最大涨幅，而债券和多数股市周一则出现下跌。此前，周末美以对伊朗的袭击引发全球市场剧烈波动。华尔街却是个例外——且出人意料地逆势上扬。【原文：Oil and gas prices clocked their biggest rise in years, while bonds and most stock markets fell on Monday, after the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran over the weekend triggered waves of volatility across world markets. The big - and surprising - exception was Wall Street.】

item.title: PayPay及其支持者计划在美国首次公开募股中筹集约11亿美元。
item.description: 日本支付应用PayPay周一宣布，该公司正与出售股份的股东共同筹备在美国进行首次公开募股（IPO），计划筹集资金高达11亿美元。该应用由软银集团支持。【原文：PayPay and a selling shareholder are aiming to ​raise as much as $1.1 billion in ‌an initial public offering in the United States, the SoftBank-backed Japanese payments app said on ​Monday.】

item.title: 巴西将在卢拉现任任期内动员近500亿美元可持续投资
item.description: 巴西政府预计在总统路易斯·伊纳西奥·卢拉·达席尔瓦当前的四年任期内，将动员超过2500亿雷亚尔（约合484亿美元）的可持续投资。前国际事务秘书塔蒂亚娜·罗西托表示，2026年将重点巩固已启动的各项举措。【原文：Brazil's government expects to mobilize more than 250 billion reais ($48.4 billion) in sustainable investments during President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva's ​current four-year term, with 2026 centered on consolidating initiatives already underway, former ‌international affairs secretary Tatiana Rosito said.】

item.title: 美国汽油价格突破每加仑3美元大关，特朗普对伊朗开战面临考验
item.description: 随着中东局势恶化，美国平均零售汽油价格周一首次突破每加仑3美元大关，分析师称这将考验公众对特朗普总统袭击伊朗决定的支持度。【原文：Average U.S. retail gasoline prices crossed $3 a gallon for the first time since November on Monday as the conflict in the Middle East ​worsened, setting up a key test of public approval of President Donald ‌Trump's decision to attack Iran, analysts said.】

item.title: 伯克希尔·哈撒韦股价在财报及CEO信函发布后下跌
item.description: 伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司周一股价创下自沃伦·巴菲特宣布卸任首席执行官以来最大跌幅，此前这家综合企业公布的财报未达部分分析师预期，并对其现金投资态度持谨慎态度。【原文：Berkshire Hathaway shares on Monday had their largest decline since Warren Buffett announced he would step down ​as chief executive, after the conglomerate posted financial results ‌that fell short of some analysts' expectations and expressed caution about investing its cash.】

item.title: 稀土企业REalloys获得五角大楼资助
item.description: 稀土企业REalloys周一宣布，已获得美国国防部价值高达170万美元的合同，用于资助设计一座加工设施，该设施将处理用于制造武器和电子设备磁铁的金属。【原文：Rare earths company REalloys has been awarded a U.S. Department of Defense contract worth up to $1.7 ​million to fund design of a processing facility ‌for metals used to make magnets for weapons and electronics, it said on Monday.】
